  "text": "The Kubernetes API deprecation policy is enforced across several layers in the kubernetes/kubernetes repository:\n\n## 1. Deprecation Warning Generation\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/deprecation/deprecation.go`** is the core enforcement file. It defines:\n- `IsDeprecated()` — checks if an API object implements `APILifecycleDeprecated()` and whether the current server version meets or exceeds the deprecation version.\n- `WarningMessage()` — generates the human-readable deprecation warning string (e.g., 'extensions/v1beta1 Ingress is deprecated in v1.14+, unavailable in v1.22+; use networking.k8s.io/v1 Ingress').\n- `RemovedRelease()` — returns the major.minor version in which the API will be/was removed.\n- `MajorMinor()` — parses version info.\n\n## 2. Warning Injection into API Routes\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go`** is where the API route registration happens. It calls `deprecation.IsDeprecated()`, `deprecation.WarningMessage()`, and `deprecation.RemovedRelease()` for each registered API type, then calls `utilwarning.AddWarningsHandler()` to wrap each route handler with automatic warning emission.\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/warning/warning.go`** defines `AddWarningsHandler()` which wraps a route function to inject warnings via `warning.AddWarning()` on every request.\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning/context.go`** defines the `Recorder` interface, `WithWarningRecorder()` to attach a recorder to the request context, and `AddWarning()` to record warnings.\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/warning.go`** defines the HTTP middleware `WithWarningRecorder()` that attaches a deduplicating recorder to every request's context and writes RFC 7234 `Warning` response headers.\n\n## 3. Metrics and Audit Annotations\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics/metrics.go`** records the `requested_deprecated_apis` Prometheus gauge and sets audit annotations `k8s.io/deprecated=true` and `k8s.io/removed-release=<version>` on requests to deprecated API versions.\n\n## 4. Removal Version Enforcement\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/deleted_kinds.go`** implements `resourceExpirationEvaluator` with:\n- `NewResourceExpirationEvaluator()` — creates evaluator with current version\n- `ShouldServeForVersion(majorRemoved, minorRemoved)` — returns false if the API's removal version is <= current server version (with alpha/beta exemptions and KUBE_APISERVER_SERVE_REMOVED_APIS_FOR_ONE_RELEASE escape hatch)\n- `RemoveDeletedKinds()` — iterates all registered storage and removes any whose `APILifecycleRemoved()` indicates they should no longer be served\n\n**`pkg/controlplane/apiserver/apis.go`** calls `NewResourceExpirationEvaluator` and `RemoveDeletedKinds` during API server startup to purge removed API versions from the API group storage map.\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/kube-aggregator/pkg/apiserver/apiserver.go`** similarly calls `NewResourceExpirationEvaluator` for the aggregated API server.\n\n## 5. CRD Deprecation\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apiserver/customresource_handler.go`** handles deprecated CRD versions: it reads the `Deprecated` and `DeprecationWarning` fields from CRD version specs, and calls `warning.AddWarning()` for requests to deprecated CRD versions. The `defaultDeprecationWarning()` function generates a default deprecation message pointing to the next non-deprecated version.\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/validation/validation.go`** validates CRD deprecation settings via `validateDeprecationWarning()`, ensuring `DeprecationWarning` is only set on deprecated versions, is not empty, and is within character limits.\n\n## 6. Minimum Supported API Version (MinCompatibilityVersion)\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/version/version.go`** defines the `EffectiveVersion` interface with `MinCompatibilityVersion()` and its `Validate()` method which enforces that `minCompatibilityVersion` is within [binaryMinor-1] for alpha builds.\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage/resource_encoding_config.go`** uses `MinCompatibilityVersion()` in `emulatedStorageVersion()` to select the appropriate storage API version for backward compatibility, skipping API versions introduced after the min-compatibility version.\n\n**`cmd/kube-apiserver/app/options/validation.go`** calls `ValidateKubeEffectiveVersion()` at startup to enforce that emulation/compatibility version settings are valid.\n\n## 7. ## API Deprecation Policy Enforcement in kubernetes/kubernetes

The policy is enforced across three main mechanisms:

### 1. Deprecated API Warning Injection
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/deprecation/deprecation.go`** — Core logic: `IsDeprecated()`, `WarningMessage()`, `RemovedRelease()`
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go`** — Wires deprecation warnings into every API route handler at registration time
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/warning/warning.go`** — `AddWarningsHandler()` wraps routes to emit warnings per-request
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning/context.go`** — Context-based `Recorder` interface and `AddWarning()`
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/warning.go`** — HTTP middleware `WithWarningRecorder()` that writes RFC 7234 `Warning` response headers
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics/metrics.go`** — `requested_deprecated_apis` Prometheus gauge + audit annotations
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apiserver/customresource_handler.go`** — CRD deprecated version warning injection with `defaultDeprecationWarning()`

### 2. Removal Version Validation
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/deleted_kinds.go`** — `ResourceExpirationEvaluator`, `RemoveDeletedKinds()`, `ShouldServeForVersion()`
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/validation/validation.go`** — Validates CRD `Deprecated`/`DeprecationWarning` fields
- **`pkg/controlplane/apiserver/apis.go`** — Calls `RemoveDeletedKinds()` at startup

### 3. Minimum Supported API Version
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/version/version.go`** — `EffectiveVersion` with `MinCompatibilityVersion()` and `Validate()`
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage/resource_encoding_config.go`** — `emulatedStorageVersion()` enforces min compatibility
- **`cmd/kube-apiserver/app/options/validation.go`** — Calls `ValidateKubeEffectiveVersion()` at startup

### Code Generator
- **`staging/src/k8s.io/code-generator/cmd/prerelease-lifecycle-gen/prerelease-lifecycle-generators/status.go`** — Generates `APILifecycleDeprecated()`, `APILifecycleRemoved()`, `APILifecycleIntroduced()` methods

    30→// WithWarningRecorder attaches a deduplicating k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning#WarningRecorder to the request context.
    31→func WithWarningRecorder(handler http.Handler) http.Handler {
    32→	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
    33→		recorder := &recorder{writer: w}
    34→		req = req.WithContext(warning.WithWarningRecorder(req.Context(), recorder))
    35→		handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)
    36→	})
    37→}
    38→
    39→var (
    40→	truncateAtTotalRunes = 4 * 1024
    41→	truncateItemRunes    = 256
    42→)
    43→
    44→type recordedWarning struct {
    45→	agent string
    46→	text  string
    47→}
    48→
    49→type recorder struct {
    50→	// lock guards calls to AddWarning from multiple threads
    51→	lock sync.Mutex
    52→
    53→	// recorded tracks whether AddWarning was already called with a given text
    54→	recorded map[string]bool
    55→
    56→	// ordered tracks warnings added so they can be replayed and truncated if needed
    57→	ordered []recordedWarning
    58→
    59→	// written tracks how many runes of text have been added as warning headers
    60→	written int
    61→
    62→	// truncating tracks if we have already exceeded truncateAtTotalRunes and are now truncating warning messages as we add them
    63→	truncating bool
    64→
    65→	// writer is the response writer to add warning headers to
    66→	writer http.ResponseWriter
    67→}
    68→
    69→func (r *recorder) AddWarning(agent, text string) {
    70→	if len(text) == 0 {
    71→		return
    72→	}
    73→
    74→	r.lock.Lock()
    75→	defer r.lock.Unlock()
    76→
    77→	// if we've already exceeded our limit and are already truncating, return early
    78→	if r.written >= truncateAtTotalRunes && r.truncating {
    79→		return
    80→	}
    81→
    82→	// init if needed
    83→	if r.recorded == nil {
    84→		r.recorded = map[string]bool{}
    85→	}
    86→
    87→	// dedupe if already warned
    88→	if r.recorded[text] {
    89→		return
    90→	}
    91→	r.recorded[text] = true
    92→	r.ordered = append(r.ordered, recordedWarning{agent: agent, text: text})
    93→
    94→	// truncate on a rune boundary, if needed
    95→	textRuneLength := utf8.RuneCountInString(text)
    96→	if r.truncating && textRuneLength > truncateItemRunes {
    97→		text = string([]rune(text)[:truncateItemRunes])
    98→		textRuneLength = truncateItemRunes
    99→	}
   100→
   101→	// compute the header
   102→	header, err := net.NewWarningHeader(299, agent, text)
   103→	if err != nil {
   104→		return
   105→	}
   106→
   107→	// if this fits within our limit, or we're already truncating, write and return
   108→	if r.written+textRuneLength <= truncateAtTotalRunes || r.truncating {
   109→		r.written += textRuneLength
   110→		r.writer.Header().Add("Warning", header)
   111→		return
   112→	}
   113→
   114→	// otherwise, enable truncation, reset, and replay the existing items as truncated warnings
   115→	r.truncating = true
   116→	r.written = 0
   117→	r.writer.Header().Del("Warning")
   118→	utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("exceeded max warning header size, truncating"))
   119→	for _, w := range r.ordered {
   120→		agent := w.agent
   121→		text := w.text
   122→
   123→		textRuneLength := utf8.RuneCountInString(text)
   124→		if textRuneLength > truncateItemRunes {
   125→			text = string([]rune(text)[:truncateItemRunes])
   126→			textRuneLength = truncateItemRunes
   127→		}
   128→		if header, err := net.NewWarningHeader(299, agent, text); err == nil {
   129→			r.written += textRuneLength
   130→			r.writer.Header().Add("Warning", header)
   131→		}
   132→	}
   133→}

    36→// This is the comment tag that carries parameters for API status generation.  Because the cadence is fixed, we can predict
    37→// with near certainty when this lifecycle happens as the API is introduced.
    38→const (
    39→	tagEnabledName    = "k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen"
    40→	introducedTagName = tagEnabledName + ":introduced"
    41→	deprecatedTagName = tagEnabledName + ":deprecated"

    47→// enabledTagValue holds parameters from a tagName tag.
    48→type tagValue struct {
    49→	value string

    51→
    52→func extractEnabledTypeTag(t *types.Type) *tagValue {
    53→	comments := append(append([]string{}, t.SecondClosestCommentLines...), t.CommentLines...)
    54→	return extractTag(tagEnabledName, comments)
    55→}
    56→
    57→func tagExists(tagName string, t *types.Type) bool {
    58→	comments := append(append([]string{}, t.SecondClosestCommentLines...), t.CommentLines...)
    59→	rawTag := extractTag(tagName, comments)
    60→	return rawTag != nil
    61→}
    62→
    63→func extractKubeVersionTag(tagName string, t *types.Type) (*tagValue, int, int, error) {
    64→	comments := append(append([]string{}, t.SecondClosestCommentLines...), t.CommentLines...)
    65→	rawTag := extractTag(tagName, comments)
    66→	if rawTag == nil || len(rawTag.value) == 0 {
    67→		return nil, -1, -1, fmt.Errorf("%v missing %v=Version tag", t, tagName)
    68→	}
    69→
    70→	splitValue := strings.Split(rawTag.value, ".")
    71→	if len(splitValue) != 2 || len(splitValue[0]) == 0 || len(splitValue[1]) == 0 {
    72→		return nil, -1, -1, fmt.Errorf("%v format must match %v=xx.yy tag", t, tagName)
    73→	}
    74→	major, err := strconv.ParseInt(splitValue[0], 10, 32)
    75→	if err != nil {
    76→		return nil, -1, -1, fmt.Errorf("%v format must match %v=xx.yy : %w", t, tagName, err)
    77→	}
    78→	minor, err := strconv.ParseInt(splitValue[1], 10, 32)
    79→	if err != nil {
    80→		return nil, -1, -1, fmt.Errorf("%v format must match %v=xx.yy : %w", t, tagName, err)
    81→	}
    82→
    83→	return rawTag, int(major), int(minor), nil
    84→}

    86→func extractIntroducedTag(t *types.Type) (*tagValue, int, int, error) {
    87→	return extractKubeVersionTag(introducedTagName, t)
    88→}

  "text": "The Kubernetes API deprecation policy is enforced across several layers in the kubernetes/kubernetes repository:\n\n## 1. Deprecation Warning Generation\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/deprecation/deprecation.go`** is the core enforcement file. It defines:\n- `IsDeprecated()` — checks if an API object implements `APILifecycleDeprecated()` and whether the current server version meets or exceeds the deprecation version.\n- `WarningMessage()` — generates the human-readable deprecation warning string (e.g., 'extensions/v1beta1 Ingress is deprecated in v1.14+, unavailable in v1.22+; use networking.k8s.io/v1 Ingress').\n- `RemovedRelease()` — returns the major.minor version in which the API will be/was removed.\n- `MajorMinor()` — parses version info.\n\n## 2. Warning Injection into API Routes\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go`** is where the API route registration happens. It calls `deprecation.IsDeprecated()`, `deprecation.WarningMessage()`, and `deprecation.RemovedRelease()` for each registered API type, then calls `utilwarning.AddWarningsHandler()` to wrap each route handler with automatic warning emission.\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/warning/warning.go`** defines `AddWarningsHandler()` which wraps a route function to inject warnings via `warning.AddWarning()` on every request.\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning/context.go`** defines the `Recorder` interface, `WithWarningRecorder()` to attach a recorder to the request context, and `AddWarning()` to record warnings.\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/warning.go`** defines the HTTP middleware `WithWarningRecorder()` that attaches a deduplicating recorder to every request's context and writes RFC 7234 `Warning` response headers.\n\n## 3. Metrics and Audit Annotations\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics/metrics.go`** records the `requested_deprecated_apis` Prometheus gauge and sets audit annotations `k8s.io/deprecated=true` and `k8s.io/removed-release=<version>` on requests to deprecated API versions.\n\n## 4. Removal Version Enforcement\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/deleted_kinds.go`** implements `resourceExpirationEvaluator` with:\n- `NewResourceExpirationEvaluator()` — creates evaluator with current version\n- `ShouldServeForVersion(majorRemoved, minorRemoved)` — returns false if the API's removal version is <= current server version (with alpha/beta exemptions and KUBE_APISERVER_SERVE_REMOVED_APIS_FOR_ONE_RELEASE escape hatch)\n- `RemoveDeletedKinds()` — iterates all registered storage and removes any whose `APILifecycleRemoved()` indicates they should no longer be served\n\n**`pkg/controlplane/apiserver/apis.go`** calls `NewResourceExpirationEvaluator` and `RemoveDeletedKinds` during API server startup to purge removed API versions from the API group storage map.\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/kube-aggregator/pkg/apiserver/apiserver.go`** similarly calls `NewResourceExpirationEvaluator` for the aggregated API server.\n\n## 5. CRD Deprecation\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apiserver/customresource_handler.go`** handles deprecated CRD versions: it reads the `Deprecated` and `DeprecationWarning` fields from CRD version specs, and calls `warning.AddWarning()` for requests to deprecated CRD versions. The `defaultDeprecationWarning()` function generates a default deprecation message pointing to the next non-deprecated version.\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/validation/validation.go`** validates CRD deprecation settings via `validateDeprecationWarning()`, ensuring `DeprecationWarning` is only set on deprecated versions, is not empty, and is within character limits.\n\n## 6. Minimum Supported API Version (MinCompatibilityVersion)\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/component-base/version/version.go`** defines the `EffectiveVersion` interface with `MinCompatibilityVersion()` and its `Validate()` method which enforces that `minCompatibilityVersion` is within [binaryMinor-1] for alpha builds.\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage/resource_encoding_config.go`** uses `MinCompatibilityVersion()` in `emulatedStorageVersion()` to select the appropriate storage API version for backward compatibility, skipping API versions introduced after the min-compatibility version.\n\n**`cmd/kube-apiserver/app/options/validation.go`** calls `ValidateKubeEffectiveVersion()` at startup to enforce that emulation/compatibility version settings are valid.\n\n## 7. Lifecycle Code Generator\n\n**`staging/src/k8s.io/code-generator/cmd/prerelease-lifecycle-gen/prerelease-lifecycle-generators/status.go`** is the code generator that reads `// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:deprecated=X.Y`, `// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:removed=X.Y`, and `// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=X.Y` tags from API type comments and generates the `APILifecycleDeprecated()`, `APILifecycleRemoved()`, and `APILifecycleIntroduced()` methods into `zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go` files for each API version package."
